key_insight: A general-purpose ACE interatomic potential can reproduce niobium’s complex behavior across diverse environments with near-DFT accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable large-scale simulations of niobium for defects, phonons, high-pressure response, and fracture, supporting materials design and failure analysis.
method: Trained an atomic cluster expansion potential on thousands of DFT niobium structures covering varied local environments.
Validated the model across multiple target properties and compared it against empirical and ML potentials, including a universal ML baseline.
Tested scalability and robustness in a near-million-atom molecular dynamics fracture simulation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports training on thousands of DFT structures and validation across phonons, high-pressure behavior, dislocation glide barriers, and a very large fracture simulation.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on how well future use cases match the DFT training distribution, especially for rare defects or extreme conditions.
paper_title: A general-purpose atomic cluster expansion interatomic potential for niobium
